compiled_state.description:
## Summary
After bug ad39 fixed reconcile-check's local-ticket loader, running `rebar reconcile --mode reconcile-check` against the healthy live store (627 bindings, all now checked, 0 orphaned) reports `in_sync: 0` with **3660 discrepancies** — i.e. EVERY binding shows some field mismatch. The authoritative real differ (dry-run) dispatches ~0 inbound changes over the same store, so these are representational diffs (status-name mapping, description/priority format, label normalization), not actionable drift.

## Reproduction Steps
1. `rebar reconcile --mode reconcile-check` on a healthy store post-ad39.
2. Observe `bridge_state/reconcile-check.json`: checked=627, orphaned=0, in_sync=0, discrepancies=3660.

Expected: in_sync ~= checked for a store the real differ considers converged.
Actual: in_sync=0; every binding flagged with field discrepancies.

## Root Cause (hypothesis)
`_compare_pair` in reconcile_check.py compares local vs Jira fields WITHOUT the same normalization the differs use (reverse status map, description ADF/markdown normalization, priority/label canonicalization). Latent until ad39 made the loader actually load tickets.

## Impact
Diagnostic-only (reconcile-check is read-only; does not act). But in_sync=0 makes the go/no-go read alarming/unusable for the class-D consumer. Should reuse the differ's field-normalization so reconcile-check's discrepancy set matches what the differ would actually dispatch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] reconcile-check's field comparison reuses the differ's normalization so a store the real differ considers converged reports in_sync ~= checked (few/zero discrepancies).
- [ ] Unit test: a binding whose normalized fields agree (but raw representations differ, e.g. status open/To Do) counts in_sync, not discrepant.
- [ ] Landed on main via Gerrit.
compiled_state.title: reconcile-check field comparison over-reports discrepancies (in_sync=0 on healthy live store)
